hero.title: Discover the Heart of Argentine Tango
hero.subtitle: Feel the Rhythm of the City

about.title: About Me
about.description:
My name is María Inés. I am a tango dancer and the creator of [BOLD]The Authentic Tango Experience[/BOLD], a cultural experience I have been developing for nearly ten years.

I fell in love with tango the very first night I stepped into a milonga. Over time, that relationship grew far beyond the dance floor. I've written a [BOOK_LINK]book of short stories[/BOOK_LINK] set in milongas, documented this world through [PHOTO_LINK]photography[/PHOTO_LINK], and [BOLD]shaped this experience[/BOLD] from a deep [BOLD]desire to share tango as a living, deeply rooted cultural expression[/BOLD].

This experience was born from a life lived inside tango.

[BOLD]My role is to guide you through the world of tango and the place where it is socially danced — the milonga —[/BOLD] with clarity and sensitivity. I explain the codes and etiquette, the music and its history, and the emotional language of the embrace as it unfolds in a real milonga, so tango becomes readable, human, accessible, and meaningful.

private.title: Cultural tango walking tour and milonga experience
private.description:
We begin by [BOLD]walking through the streets of Buenos Aires[/BOLD], where tango was born — uncovering its [BOLD]history[/BOLD], its poets, its music, and the neighborhoods that gave it life. As we explore the city on foot, tango reveals itself not only as a dance, but as a profound cultural expression rooted in everyday life.

The tour culminates in a [BOLD]visit to an authentic milonga[/BOLD], where we witness [BOLD]social tango[/BOLD] as it truly is: spontaneous, intimate, and rich in codes and traditions. Watching the dancers and decoding the etiquette with the [BOLD]guidance of a local expert[/BOLD] turns the experience into something [BOLD]uniquely insightful[/BOLD].

[BOLD]More than a show, this is a deep, guided immersion into the living culture of tango.[/BOLD]
private.includes: Included: English & Spanish guide, Glass of wine/soda/coffee, milonga entry

group.title: Cultural tango walking tour and milonga experience + group class
group.description:
We begin by [BOLD]walking through the streets of Buenos Aires[/BOLD], where tango was born, discovering its [BOLD]history[/BOLD], music, poets, and the neighborhoods that shaped it. As we move through the city, tango reveals itself not just as a dance, but as a way of living and feeling Buenos Aires.

The experience continues with a [BOLD]visit to an authentic milonga[/BOLD] — the place where tango is truly alive. Here, participants are invited to step into a relaxed and fun group class, an easy and entertaining way to feel tango through movement and connection, no previous experience needed. After the class, [BOLD]social tango[/BOLD] unfolds in its most intimate and spontaneous form, full of codes, rituals, and unspoken rules that we learn to read with the [BOLD]help of a local expert[/BOLD].

[BOLD]More than watching tango, this is a guided immersion into the living culture of tango.[/BOLD]
group.includes: Included: English & Spanish guide, Glass of wine/soda/coffee, milonga entry, group class

customizedTours.title: Customized Tours
customizedTours.description:
An [BOLD]immersive cultural proposal[/BOLD] in Buenos Aires, centered on [BOLD]tango as a living social and cultural expression[/BOLD].

The experience invites participants to discover one of the most representative expressions of [BOLD]porteño identity[/BOLD] through historical context and direct contact with [BOLD]authentic tango spaces: the milongas[/BOLD]. Depending on the group’s interests, the program may include a [BOLD]shared or private tango class[/BOLD], offering an approachable and memorable way to engage with the experience.

More than a tourist activity, this proposal encourages [BOLD]cultural exchange[/BOLD], curiosity, and connection, making tango accessible and enjoyable, even for those who do not dance.

Designed for [BOLD]educational institutions, universities, corporate groups, international delegations, and cultural exchange programs[/BOLD], the experience is [BOLD]flexible and adaptable[/BOLD] in duration, depth, and format.

An [BOLD]authentic cultural encounter[/BOLD] that allows participants to understand Buenos Aires from the inside and connect with tango, its [BOLD]most vibrant expression[/BOLD].

booking.title: Book Your Tour
booking.description: Select a date and time that works for you

ebook.title: Discover the inner world of tango through my book Entre La Patagonia y el Tango
ebook.description: Born from a [BOLD]hilarious anecdote[/BOLD], an [BOLD]uncontrollable need to dance tango[/BOLD], and a [BOLD]deep longing for the milonga[/BOLD] while living far away in [BOLD]Patagonia[/BOLD], these stories became my [BOLD]milonguero survival kit[/BOLD]: while my feet walked along [BOLD]Lake Nahuel Huapi[/BOLD], my mind, in [BOLD]high heels[/BOLD], lingered on the [BOLD]dance floors of Buenos Aires[/BOLD], carried by [BOLD]music and embrace[/BOLD].

reviews.name: Ana
reviews.message: Best experience we participated in Buenos Aires (better than the nice tourist tango shows, or nice estancia tours and cooking classes). The stories about how and why tango appeared and flourished were put in perspective by the real today's milonga - seeing how the locals live and breathe the tango today, with it's rituals and amazing atmosphere. I am a shy introverted person who never went to a dance class, and I can't wait to start learning tango when I go home. Highly recommend.

reviews.name: Murray.
reviews.message: Even if you are remotely interested in dance, this is a must do. Maria’s knowledge and passion shines through making it the best experience we have had in the cities we have traveled so far. Very authentic, as this is where the locals are, doing their thing on a weekday afternoon, and so explained by our beautiful host.

reviews.name: Amanda.
reviews.message: Marie is incredibly knowledgeable about the history and origin of Buenos Aires Tango and explained this in a captivating, humorous and interactive way. We had several stops where we learnt not only about Tango history but also a few basic tango moves - all interspersed with information about the Theatre District and favourite & typical Argentinian snacks & cakes. We finished off at a Malinga (where locals go to Tango) where we were invited to dance and make some new Buenos Aires friends who patiently taught us some new moves! Unbelievable experience and thoroughly recommend. You do not need to have any dance experience to enjoy. Thank you Marie!

reviews.name: Luca
reviews.message: Maria is an excellent tango guide. Not only she is very knowledgeable about the history of tango, she is an expert and passionate tango dancer herself.

reviews.name: Xiaolei
reviews.message: Due to the weather, I was the only person who participated in the experience today, so it became a private group. Maria is very meticulous about the historical origins and the rise and fall of tango, and she gave me a lot of information, which was eye-opening. I also really enjoyed the tango steps and the method she taught me on the street. Finally, we went dancing. The amazing thing is that, as a beginner, I received two invitations, which made my overall experience very complete. Thank you. Highly recommended.

reviews.name: Heidi bech Geschwendtner
reviews.message: I had an amazing day with Maria on a private tour and learned so much about the rich and fascinating world of tango. Her knowledge is deep and shared in such a natural, engaging way. We had a lot of fun, laughed a lot, and truly enjoyed every moment. Maria is not only incredibly knowledgeable but also a wonderful person to be guided by. Thank you for a fantastic experience — I highly recommend Maria.

reviews.name: Thomas Obel
reviews.message: I really enjoyed our private day with Maria. She is very knowledgeable, easy to talk to, and made tango history come alive with great stories. A fun, relaxed, and excellent experience. My warmest recommendations. Best Regards Thomas

reviews.name: Flor Arbiser
reviews.message: I joined María Inés's Tango Tour just to accompany tourists. But it turned out to be incredibly entertaining for me, a Buenos Aires native and hardly any tango enthusiast. María Inés knows the history of tango so deeply that she makes you feel like you're part of its origins, as if you were in a bar in La Boca at the beginning of the last century. She speaks perfect, almost musical English. To top it all off, she teaches a few steps with ease and charm, and takes you inside authentic milongas. There, María Inés puts on her heels and proves herself to be one of the best dancers.

reviews.name: Lisa Bryzek
reviews.message: María was a wonderful guide! I brought my mother in law and son to Buenos Aires to dance tango since my son has been taking tango lessons. I wanted a more local experience but was intimidated to go into a milonga by ourselves. I found Maria on Airbnb experiences and she was very easy to communicate with. We first learned about the history of tango and then She brought us to a milonga and taught us the milonga etiquette. Maria took my son out on the dance floor to “break the ice” and from that moment on my son was asked to dance all night long by both men and women and had a blast! Thank you Maria for this wonderful local experience! I highly recommend!!

reviews.name: Tulip Order Office
reviews.message: Last December my family had such a wonderful evening with Maria Ines. We learnt all about the history of the tango, then visited a tango hall to watch some amazing dancing. It was one of the most memorable times for me. Thank you and love from Bill, John, Milo, Teo and Otto
